title: pilosella viscidula
intro: Pilosella viscidula, also known as sticky mouse-ear, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its hairy, sticky leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Pilosella viscidula include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin issues. Herbal preparations of Pilosella viscidula can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed internally to reap its benefits.
